text:
A Wooden Vault (, Kidzukurigura) is an old type of camouflaged safe. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 10, Chapter 86 

Appearance

A wooden vault's appearance probably varies. The one acquired by Gon has the form of a robed woman. 

Overview

Appraisers and forgers look at three things when examining a wooden vault: the container, the glue in the seam, and the treasure inside. The container is hard to appraise because the carving is usually crude and quality does not serve as a basis for authenticity. The seam, hidden with wood shavings and paint, can have its discoloration examined. However, it can be difficult for even professionals to spot heat discoloration if cauterization was used to re-glue the seam. 

Due to the potential value of the content inside, wooden vaults are the target of fraudsters using several doctoring methods and/or tricks:
Transplant(s):
Replace all jewelry with fakes.
Leave some of the real jewelry.
Bait with real jewelry in displays, then switch to counterfeits right before a sale.
Replace with genuine but lower quality, contemporary jewelry.
Replace with genuine but lower quality, period jewelry.
Facelift: Use old wood to carve a fake period vault.
Cauterization: Melt the (old, oxidized) glue in the seam and reuse it to reseal the vault (instead of using fresh glue), giving the impression of inviolability.
Autopsy: Display a real, opened vault with its original contents replaced by cheap jewelry.
Ostomy: Make a hole away from the seam, take the jewelry out through it, replace the jewelry with fakes, and patch up the hole. 

Background

Wooden vaults were mostly used by wealthy individuals 300 years ago for tax evasion purposes. 

Plot

Yorknew City arc

Using Gyo to spot "residual aura" and find valuable items at the Bull Market, Gon and Killua purchase a wooden vault with a winning bid of 7,000. They take it along with some other items to an appraiser who analyzes it and claims that it's only worth 1,500. However, he compliments the quality of the wood and states that aged wood is a treasure to modern sculptors. Right as the appraiser begins to take the wooden statue into his backroom to "analyze" the quality of the wood, Zepile arrives in the shop and stops him. He reveals that the most important part of the vault are its contents and explains the vault's history in detail. 

After Gon, Killua, and Zepile enjoy lunch together, Zepile opens up the wooden vault and reveals the treasure inside that emanates aura. Later on, they take the vault and its contents to the Preview Market and the moment it's displayed, a couple of men try to question its authenticity in order to drive down the price. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 10, Chapter 87 Hunter × Hunter - Volume 10, Chapter 88 On the day of the Preview Market auction, the wooden vault and its contents sell for 355 million. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 11, Chapter 103 

Trivia

In the translation of the 2011 anime adaptation, the item is referred to as "wooden trove". Hunter × Hunter - Episode 48 (2011) 

Anime and Manga Differences

While in the manga the vault shown is shaped like a robed woman, in the 1999 anime adaptation it has no distinguishable form Hunter × Hunter - Episode 57 (1999) and in the 2011 anime adaptation it resembles a seated Buddha-like figure. 

Intertextuality and References

The wooden vault concept is similar to and may have been based on the real-life "santo do pau oco" ("hollow wood saint") or other similar concepts around the world. According to popular Brazilian lore, these statues of saints were used in mining regions during the colonial period to smuggle gold or diamond dust and fool tax authorities, but with little proof, this is taken as a legend. This kind of image was probably made for the same reasons as in Europe where, since the Middle Ages, wood carvings were hollowed out so that the pieces would crack less and become lighter.
